title: Graphastics Exploration
summary: Graphic statics is a structural analysis methodology that interprets equilibrium conditions through geometric representations of forces and moments, employing visualization tools including free body diagrams, force polygons, and moment distribution diagrams. This parametric design framework implements computational graphic statics principles through Grasshopper's algorithmic modeling environment, enabling force-driven generation of structurally informed architectural morphologies. The development process systematically integrates spatial scales, functional requirements, and material constraints to achieve equilibrium-optimized configurations with verified constructability.

title: Decentralized post-war shelter generative architecture
summary: The reconstruction of Palestinian shelters amid the ongoing Israel-Palestine conflict requires a sustainable, efficient, and resilient approach. By integrating hybrid generative design—leveraging machine learning (ML) and heuristic algorithms—we propose a novel framework for optimizing shelter design. This approach balances structural stability, material efficiency, environmental adaptation, and rapid deployment to ensure safe and habitable spaces for displaced communities.

title: Intelligent Building Exhibition & Exchange Center
summary: Driven by parametric algorithms, this project achieves the collaborative generation of large-span spaces and structural systems. Deeply integrating BIM platforms and finite element analysis (FEA), the design transforms complex spatial grids into standardized modular units. The scheme fully adopts prefabricated assembly technology, linking data models directly to component prefabrication and rapid on-site assembly, thereby realizing a highly efficient transition from digital morphogenesis to smart construction.

title: AI-driven Generative Aerial Hub
summary: Visioning the Future Low-Altitude Economy: Design of UAV-Prefabricated High-Rise Office Buildings

title: GATS: A Predictive System for Street Safety and Pedestrian Vitality in Generative Urban Design
summary: This project develops an integrated workflow that connects urban sensing, graph neural networks, and parametric design. Using 220 urban samples from Manhattan and Brooklyn, it combines GIS road networks, Google Street View imagery, semantic segmentation, facade material recognition, pedestrian detection, and crime data to construct a two-layer hierarchical graph of street segments, intersections, and observation points. A GATv2-based graph neural network is trained to predict street-level safety and pedestrian vitality. The model is then embedded into a Rhino/Grasshopper generative design workflow, enabling early-stage design alternatives to receive rapid social-performance feedback for multi-objective optimization and urban decision-making.

title: Heritage Building Information Modelling in Group Portrait Depiction of Regional Historical Wooden Buildings: from Beiyuyuan Five-Mountain Temple to Fenyang
summary: This study presents an HBIM workflow to create a parametric component library for Fenyang's Yuan Dynasty timber buildings. Using the Water-God Hall as a case study, we fused TLS, UAV, and measurement data into precise point clouds. A reusable library modeled in Rhino/Grasshopper was transferred to Revit via Rhino.Inside to integrate non-geometric data. Through parameter adjustments, the model efficiently adapts to similar structures like the Five-Mountain Hall. This digital conservation pipeline provides a scalable resource for comparative architectural and regional structural analysis.

title: Collaborative Optimization of Morphological Performance in Residential Districts Using Interpretable Machine Learning: a case study area in Changsha
summary: This study addresses the challenge of balancing energy efficiency and thermal comfort in high-density residential designs. Using field surveys and simulations, we developed a surrogate model (R² = 0.91) that significantly accelerates multi-objective optimization. Additionally, we introduce an interactive Streamlit platform with Pareto-front analysis to visualize design trade-offs, effectively translating advanced optimization into practical workflows for sustainable urban planning.

title: The University Innovation Center: Interior Renovation Design Project
summary: 该项目是我作为校方主设计师，沟通协调施工方完成的学校大创中心改造落地项目

title: Autonomous Unmanned Inclined Masonry of Discrete Shells Using Aerial Robots
summary: 该项目为CDAD_2024计算性设计工作营的落地项目，为无人机搭建双曲马鞍面模块的一次尝试，我在此次工作营获得优秀营员称号
